findings.description: Module 'knowledge_graph_v2' has API but no backend contract test
findings.issue_explanation: API-First Contract Testing requires HTTP-level tests. One API test validates the entire call chain implicitly (Controller → Service → Repository → Database).
findings.impact_estimate: Prevents regression in API contracts (60-300x faster than browser tests)

findings.issue_explanation: The @pytest.mark.api_contract marker identifies tests that validate API contracts. This allows selective test execution and coverage reporting.

agent_reports.summary: TEST COVERAGE ISSUES: 2 finding(s) in 1 module(s) - 1 CRITICAL, 1 HIGH, 0 MEDIUM, 0 LOW (2 API contract gap(s), 0 general coverage gap(s))
✅ Module 'knowledge_graph_v2' fully compliant with Module Federation Standard v1.0

findings.description: N+1 query pattern: database call at line 227 inside loop
findings.recommendation: Use bulk query, JOIN, or prefetch to avoid N queries
findings.code_snippet: for node_key, node_label, node_type, properties_json in cursor.fetchall():
findings.code_snippet_with_context:
225 |             """, (ontology_id,))
   226 | 
   227 |             for node_key, node_label, node_type, properties_json in cursor.fetchall():  # 🔴
   228 |                 # Parse properties
   229 |                 properties = json.loads(properties_json) if properties_json else {}
   230 | 
   231 |                 # Map node_type string to enum
   232 |                 node_type_enum = self._parse_node_type(node_type)
   233 | 
   234 |                 # Create and add node
   235 |                 node = GraphNode(node_key, node_label, node_type_enum, properties)
   236 |                 graph.add_node(node)
   237 | 
   238 |             # Load edges
findings.issue_explanation: Loop executes database call N times (once per iteration). For 1000 items: 1000 individual queries instead of 1 bulk query. Impact: Slow performance, increased database load, network overhead.
findings.fix_example:
# Option 1: List comprehension (Pythonic, 15-20% faster)
rows = cursor.fetchall()
items = [
    process_item(r[0], r[1], parse_type(r[2]), json.loads(r[3]) if r[3] else {})
    for r in rows
]

# Option 2: Cached lookup with @lru_cache (20-30% faster)
from functools import lru_cache

@lru_cache(maxsize=128)
def parse_type(type_str: str):
    # ... parsing logic ...
    return result

# Then use in loop (cache prevents repeated parsing)
for row in cursor.fetchall():
    item_type = parse_type(row[2])  # Cached after first call
findings.impact_estimate: 10-100x speedup (depends on data size, typically 50-90% faster)

findings.description: N+1 query pattern: database call at line 245 inside loop
findings.recommendation: Use bulk query, JOIN, or prefetch to avoid N queries
findings.code_snippet: for from_key, to_key, edge_type, edge_label, properties_json in cursor.fetchall():
findings.code_snippet_with_context:
243 |             """, (ontology_id,))
   244 | 
   245 |             for from_key, to_key, edge_type, edge_label, properties_json in cursor.fetchall():  # 🔴
   246 |                 # Parse properties
   247 |                 properties = json.loads(properties_json) if properties_json else {}
   248 | 
   249 |                 # Map edge_type string to enum
   250 |                 edge_type_enum = self._parse_edge_type(edge_type)
   251 | 
   252 |                 # Create and add edge
   253 |                 edge = GraphEdge(from_key, to_key, edge_type_enum, edge_label, properties)
   254 |                 graph.add_edge(edge)
   255 | 
   256 |             logger.info(f"Loaded graph '{graph_type.value}': {len(graph.nodes)} nodes, {len(graph.edges)} edges")
findings.issue_explanation: Loop executes database call N times (once per iteration). For 1000 items: 1000 individual queries instead of 1 bulk query. Impact: Slow performance, increased database load, network overhead.
findings.fix_example:
# Option 1: List comprehension (Pythonic, 15-20% faster)
rows = cursor.fetchall()
items = [
    process_item(r[0], r[1], parse_type(r[2]), json.loads(r[3]) if r[3] else {})
    for r in rows
]

# Option 2: Cached lookup with @lru_cache (20-30% faster)
from functools import lru_cache

@lru_cache(maxsize=128)
def parse_type(type_str: str):
    # ... parsing logic ...
    return result

# Then use in loop (cache prevents repeated parsing)
for row in cursor.fetchall():
    item_type = parse_type(row[2])  # Cached after first call
findings.impact_estimate: 10-100x speedup (depends on data size, typically 50-90% faster)

findings.recommendation: Use constructor injection or parameter passing for db_path
findings.code_snippet_with_context:
36 |             db_path: Path to SQLite database
    37 |                     Example: 'modules/knowledge_graph_v2/database/graph_cache.db'
    38 |         """

    40 |         logger.info(f"SqliteGraphCacheRepository initialized: {db_path}")
    41 |         self._ensure_schema()
    42 |
findings.issue_explanation: Accessing .db_path creates tight coupling and prevents testing. Components cannot be tested with mocks, and changing implementations requires modifying all call sites. Violates Dependency Inversion Principle (SOLID).
findings.impact_estimate: Improved testability (100% mockable), loose coupling, easier refactoring
findings.gof_pattern_rationale: Encapsulates complex object creation, enables testing with mocks
